arguments.task: Open official Twilio pages needed to verify structured research fields. Read and extract from canonical first-party pages only: 1) a Twilio developer/API overview page describing Twilio APIs/products, 2) an official authentication/security page that documents all supported API auth methods for Twilio APIs such as API keys, Account SID/Auth Token with HTTP Basic, OAuth if documented, 3) an official pricing or signup page showing whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid, 4) if available an official page about MCP/server integrations. Return concise notes with exact supporting URLs actually visited and the key evidence for description, auth methods, access tier, API surface type/breadth, MCP existence, rate limits, and buildability. Avoid search snippets; browse page content directly.

From Twilio Docs, identify and open canonical documentation pages supporting: API overview/surface, authentication methods, rate limits, and pricing/access if linked from official Twilio properties. Extract exact statements and URLs. Include whether Twilio exposes REST APIs, GraphQL, or both; breadth of APIs/products; all documented auth methods found on first-party pages; and any official MCP/server page if it exists. Return only evidence-backed findings.

raw_result.description: Cloud communications platform offering APIs for messaging, voice, video, email, contact center, and customer data workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Twilio docs for API requests document HTTP Basic authentication using either Account SID + Auth Token or API Key + API Secret; Twilio also documents API Keys with Main, Standard, and Restricted key types. No first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2 as a general Twilio API auth method in this pass.
raw_result.gating_reason: Twilio pricing page advertises 'Start for free' and self-serve pay-as-you-go pricing.

api_surface.breadth: Broad communications platform API surface spanning messaging, voice, video, conversations, email, contact center, and related products; official MCP page references 1,800+ endpoints across 30+ products.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Twilio has an official Model Context Protocol server in public beta, documented on Twilio Docs.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Evidence gathered in this pass indicates Twilio has product-specific rate/concurrency limits, but no canonical rate-limit page URL was verified from the already-read pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Comprehensive first-party docs, official SDKs, self-serve pricing, and an official MCP server are strong signals for agent toolkit buildability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured auth_methods field is somewhat ambiguous because the gathered evidence confirms HTTP Basic with Account SID/Auth Token and API Key/API Secret, but the allowed schema distinguishes 'basic' and 'token' while Twilio's docs present these credentials within Basic auth. Rate-limit specifics were also not tied to a verified canonical rate-limit page in the gathered evidence.
